content.full_text:
Novoa
Jewelers
Since 1973

FPPC
Mar 2 10:00 AM '90

Bob & Patty Novoa

April 26, 1990

Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J. St.
Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Mr. Jonathan Rothman
Counsel Legal Division FPPC

Dear Mr. Rothman,

Enclosed you will find a copy of a public notice which
will be published in the Newman Index on May 3, 1990.

At this time, I would like to request a ruling under
regulation 18703.5 where as Mr. Robert Mendonca has in the
preceding twelve months, maintained an open charge account
with our business and has made purchases in excess of one
tenth of one percent of our gross sales. Upon reviewing
our records, we have determined that our gross sales represent
approximately twenty-five percent of the households of the
jurisdiction.

If any further information is required, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

With my sincere appreciation,

Robert G. Novoa
Planning Commissioner
City of Newman

Enclosure

gm

940 Fresno Street • Newman, CA 95360 • (209) 862-2680

This image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page, possibly the back of a document. There are two small circular marks or stamps visible on the lower portion of the page, but no legible text content to transcribe.

City of Newman

CITY HALL
P.O. BOX 787
NEWMAN, CALIFORNIA 95360
(209) 862-3725


PUBLIC NOTICE

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN the City of Newman Planning Commission will
hold a public hearing on Thursday, May 17, 1990 at 7:00 P.M., or as
soon thereafter as it may be heard, to consider the following item:

Use Permit 1-90-UP, filed by Robert Mendonca, to allow a fence
in the C-2 District which exceeds six (6) feet in height.

The property is located at 887 N street (APN 26-040-043)

The subject proposal has been determined to be exempt from the
requirements specified by the California Environmental Quality Act
of 1970, as amended.

At the above time and place the Planning Commission will consider
testimony from interested persons and other relevant information
prior to taking any action.

Subsequent challenges to Planning Commission action may be
restricted to those issues discussed during the public hearing or
Commission deliberations.

Information regarding this Item may be obtained by contacting the
City of Newman Planning Department, City Hall, 1162 'O' Street
Newman, 95360.

Brian Foucht
Planning Director
PUBLISH: MAY 3, 1990

[The image appears to be a mostly blank page with only a small tan/beige colored stain or mark in the center-left portion of the page. There is no readable text to transcribe.]
